danger_signals.visual_form: On-screen hostile entities that player characters can be damaged by
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat requiring avoidance or defeat via trash weapon or Monster Manual scare ability

danger_signals.visual_form: Final level boss, primarily Simon the Monster Hunter in endgame
danger_signals.meaning: Major danger threshold; requires resource management (Monster Manuals) and strategic use of character abilities

danger_signals.visual_form: Limited ammunition (trash, triple trash from collected sacks/bones), limited Monster Manual charges
danger_signals.meaning: Vulnerability state; player must manage consumables or face encounters under-equipped

safety_signals.visual_form: Starting area and progression hub locations
safety_signals.meaning: Non-hostile environment where Gromble gives instructions and exam objectives

safety_signals.visual_form: Ability to swap between Ickis, Oblina, or Krumm at will
safety_signals.meaning: Safety valve for puzzle-solving; each character's unique ability (flight, reach, scanning) provides tactical advantage

safety_signals.visual_form: Garbage sacks, fish bones, Monster Manuals as collectibles
safety_signals.meaning: Resource acquisition for combat readiness and special scare abilities

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Sewer starting zone; progression through human world locales above ground

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Specific people designated as 'scare targets' by Gromble
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Generic human enemies; specialized threat in form of Simon the Monster Hunter (sworn enemy)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Locales 'above ground' in human civilization; final boss arena context

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Boss-tier entity with distinct threat signature
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Final level encounter; represents culmination of Monster Midterm Exam

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Progression through sewer → human world locales above ground; level-based structure
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Gromble assigns specific scare targets; monsters must locate and scare designated humans; boss encounters escalate

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Scattered collectibles (garbage sacks, fish bones, Monster Manuals, specific items per level)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must find all items to pass exam; incomplete collection blocks progression or full graduation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Environmental obstacles requiring Ickis flight, Oblina ledge-reach, or Krumm off-screen scanning
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mandatory character switching to overcome level design; team moves (stacking) for unreachable items

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ammo availability (standard trash vs. triple trash); Monster Manual inventory
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Scare ability activation defeats all on-screen enemies or damages boss; resource depletion forces tactical retreat or puzzle alternatives

environmental_storytelling.example: Starting in monster sewer establishes underground safe zone; ascending into human world creates tonal shift from education to real-world test
Gromble instructs scare of specific people; reinforces academy authority structure and exam narrative; player must seek them out in each locale
Generic human enemies throughout levels; Simon the Monster Hunter appears as final boss, signifying narrative climax and 'sworn enemy' status
Collecting all level items required for graduation; doubles as exploration incentive and world-state validation (mastery of environment)

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Can perform short flight; primary character for airborne puzzle access; switching between characters required for level completion
Can reach high ledges; essential for vertical navigation; team stacking enables item access
Can scan off-screen areas; detective/surveillance role; reveals hidden threats or collectibles
Assigns scare targets; instructs exam objectives; stationary in academy hub
Defeated by trash weapons or Monster Manual scare ability; running behavior when spooked indicates scare success
Final boss; requires sustained Monster Manual use or strategic damage via trash; dedicated enemy AI with health pool

notes: Source data is limited (Wikipedia article) and does not contain specific visual asset descriptions, color palettes, or detailed sprite information. Manifestation patterns extracted are inferred from gameplay mechanics and narrative structure. Environmental storytelling is subtle and mechanic-driven rather than asset-heavy. The game's visual communication relies heavily on level progression geography and character ability symbology rather than explicit visual signaling (color coding, icons, HUD elements). World-state is communicated primarily through objective markers and item availability rather than dynamic environmental changes.
